description_long: This is a classic wooden barrel with a slightly bulging cylindrical shape, constructed from vertical wooden staves bound together by two wide, metallic hoops—one near the top and one near the bottom. The wood has a rich, medium-brown color with visible grain and subtle weathering, suggesting outdoor or heavy-duty use. The metallic bands are a dull gray, appearing slightly worn and matte, providing structural reinforcement. The top of the barrel is flat and made of the same wood as the sides, with no visible openings or spouts, indicating it is sealed. There are no handles or decorative elements, and the surface is generally rough and utilitarian. The overall appearance is robust and functional, suitable for storage or transport of goods in urban or industrial settings.
description: A robust, medium-brown wooden barrel with two wide, dull gray metal hoops and a flat, sealed wooden top. The surface is rough and utilitarian, with no handles or markings.
description_view_0: Front view shows the bulging wooden staves and both metal hoops clearly, with the wood grain and weathering most visible.
description_view_1: This side view is similar to the front, but the wood grain appears slightly different and the metal hoops are equally prominent.
description_view_2: Another rotated view, showing the same features with minor variations in wood texture and lighting on the metal bands.
description_view_3: Final view highlights the curvature of the barrel and the consistent appearance of the wood and metal hoops, with no additional features.
